title: podman: support to specify the namespace to join using the ns:/PATH notation
body:
add a prefix `--net ns:/PATH/TO/THE/NAMESPACE` that can be used to specify what namespace must be joined.

We probably want to do the same thing for other namespaces

comments.body: @mheon I might have misunderstood your previous comment about it and how to take rid of it, but I still think we want the extra configuration in `libpod/container.go` so that it can be used when the container is restarted.

Why do we need to store the information separately when it's in the OCI
spec? All the namespaces and their paths are stored in there, and we'll use
the same base spec every time the container restarts. I don't think we need
to retain the namespace information twice, we just need to make sure that
none of the options that would replace the network namespace with one
podman sets up are set.

On Tue, Jul 24, 2018, 07:24 Tom Sweeney <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> LGTM

@mheon I've pushed a new version where that information is stored only in the OCI specs.  If this pattern looks good, I am going to add the same feature to other namespaces
@giuseppe Yeah, this way looks fine, thanks!
BTW This was a heavily requested feature of Docker that was always rejected. Nice work @giuseppe
I've changed it to work also for the UTS, IPC, PID and userNS.
